name: 7 Layer Brownie Ice Cream Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Trace an 8-inch circle on a piece of waxed paper using a cake pan as a guide. Place the traced circles on top of six more pieces of waxed paper.
2. Cut through all layers of waxed paper, resulting in seven individual waxed paper rounds.
3. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Prepare two cookie sheets and an 8-inch springform pan.
4. In a small bowl, whisk together cocoa powder and water until blended and smooth.
5. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, beat butter and sugar for about 3 minutes until pale and fluffy.
6. Beat in the cocoa mixture (batter may appear curdled) while mixing on low speed, gradually adding flour until well combined (batter will be very stiff).
7. Place the waxed paper rounds on a countertop. Drop a level tablespoon of batter into the center of each round. Divide the remaining tablespoon of batter among the rounds (about 2 teaspoons each).
8. Moisten a cookie sheet with water and place two waxed paper rounds on it to prevent slipping.
9. Spread the batter almost to the edges of the rounds.
10. Bake for 8 minutes or until the surface looks dry and set but is slightly springy to the touch (while the first batch bakes, prepare the next two layers).
11. Cool baked layers on the cookie sheet with a wire rack for 3 minutes before transferring them to waxed paper on a wire rack.
12. Allow layers to cool completely.
13. Prepare the remaining layers by repeating steps 2-11.
14. Line an 8-inch springform pan with plastic wrap, leaving enough extend past the sides of the pan to cover the top when filled.
15. Peel off the waxed paper from the cooled brownie layers and place one layer in the bottom of the pan.
16. Spread a cup of ice cream evenly over the brownie layer.
17. Top with another brownie layer, then spread another cup of ice cream.
18. Repeat steps 15-17 with five more brownie layers and ice cream, finishing with a brownie layer on top.
19. Fold the plastic wrap over the top and freeze for at least 8 hours or until tightly wrapped and stored in the freezer for up to one month.
20. To serve, remove the sides of the pan and peel away the plastic wrap from the sides. Lift the cake off the plastic wrap onto a serving plate.
